description: Rounds 1-5: Surface-level improvements to both skill files. Fix unclear wording, add missing context, fix structural problems, improve readability. Personas: First-time AI creator, Experienced prompt engineer, Non-native English speaker, AI agent literally following instructions, Someone comparing to Cursor rules.
steps: Round 1 (First-time AI creator persona): Read both skills/relentless-iteration/SKILL.md and skills/ux-audit/SKILL.md as someone who has never used structured AI skills before. Fix: unclear jargon without explanation, missing 'what is this?' context, confusing entry points, assumed knowledge. Add a brief 'What This Skill Does' section if missing.

Round 2 (Experienced prompt engineer persona): Read both files looking for ambiguity that would cause different AI models to interpret instructions differently. Fix: vague verbs ('consider', 'think about'), unclear scope boundaries, missing explicit constraints, instructions that could be interpreted multiple ways.

Round 3 (Non-native English speaker persona): Simplify complex sentences, replace idioms with direct language, ensure technical terms are defined on first use. Fix Chinese trigger phrases that lack explanation for non-Chinese speakers. Ensure bilingual elements are inclusive not excluding.

Round 4 (AI agent literal execution persona): Read as a machine that can only do what is explicitly stated. Fix: implicit steps that assume reasoning, 'use your judgment' gaps, missing fallback instructions, unclear when to stop vs continue, ambiguous conditionals.

Round 5 (Comparing to Cursor rules/Copilot instructions persona): Assess competitive positioning. Fix: anything that feels less precise than .cursorrules format, missing trigger specificity, unclear integration points, areas where competing frameworks handle better.
acceptance_criteria: Both skill files have clear opening sections explaining what the skill does and who it's for
All Chinese text has English context/explanation alongside it
Zero instances of vague instructions like 'consider' or 'think about' without explicit actions
All trigger patterns are explicit and unambiguous
An AI agent reading either file can determine exactly what to do in the first 10 lines
verification: PYENV_VERSION=3.12.13 python -m pytest tests/ -q passes with no new failures
Both files maintain valid YAML frontmatter
Both files are well-structured markdown that renders correctly
findings: Pre-existing test failure in test_knowledge.py::TestSkillPathResolution::test_skill_paths_resolve (expects 28 skills in index but 29 exist). Not caused by this change. 1025 tests pass, 1 pre-existing failure. Both files have valid YAML frontmatter confirmed via Python yaml.safe_load.
